selected_important_turn.text: I'm afraid that will not work for me. I have hypothyroidism, so I am intolerant to cold temperatures. I was also planning on taking a swim in the river. I would prefer to take two firewood & one food package, & you may have the three waters, two food packages, & one firewood.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first substantive counter-offer from YOU and sets the early tone. By responding to THEM's take-all offer with a firm, self-oriented counter-offer, YOU helped lock the negotiation into a competitive, zero-sum framing instead of opening integrative possibilities.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It is overly positional and assertive: it defends a desired allocation without exploring the opponent's constraints or searching for tradeoffs. That both parties have medical needs for the same scarce item means a collaborative diagnostic approach or creative trades would likely yield better joint outcomes; this turn missed that chance.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of issuing a fixed counter-split, briefly acknowledge the opponent's need and invite tradeoffs or brainstorm integrative options (e.g., ask what they can trade besides items, propose splitting the wood three ways or pairing wood with location/behavioral solutions). Offer one modest concession conditionally rather than a take-it-or-leave-it split.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: likely increase — opening brainstorming/conditional concessions would reveal integrative options and create mutually beneficial trades
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — avoiding a hard positional stance leads to a more balanced, perceived-fair split
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — collaborative probing reduces defensiveness and speeds toward a workable solution
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — a less adversarial tone builds trust and reduces tension

value2reason.Low: Even when I'm not camping, I have a 32-pack of water bottles in my car's trunk. I'd prefer to have some extra, though, you can never be too hydrated (well, you can, but you get my drift).
value2reason.Medium: I need food for energy, of course, & I was also planning on catching some crawdad in the river (to keep, not to eat), so I will need bait for them. All I brought with me is a few protein bars.
value2reason.High: I need firewood to keep warm, as I have hypothyroidism, which makes me intolerant of the cold. I was also planning on going for a swim in the river.

value2reason.Low: I have adequate water but emergencies always happen.
value2reason.Medium: I am feeling under the weather and need to eat additional food to feel well.
value2reason.High: I have anaemia and would need the firewood to make sure I stay warm.

dialogue.text: how about I give you 2 waters and 1 food package in exchange for all your firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm afraid that will not work for me. I have hypothyroidism, so I am intolerant to cold temperatures. I was also planning on taking a swim in the river. I would prefer to take two firewood & one food package, & you may have the three waters, two food packages, & one firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for firewood, but I also need it for warmth. What if we split the firewood and trade some food for water? That way, we both get what we need without taking all the wood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your suggestion, but I need more firewood to stay warm due to my anaemia. How about I take 2 firewood, 1 food, and 1 water, and you take the remaining 1 firewood, 2 food, and 2 water? That way, we both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your need for more firewood, but I have hypothyroidism and need it for warmth too. What if I take 2 firewood, 2 food, and 1 water, and you take 1 firewood, 1 food, and 2 water? That way, we both get enough for our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your concern about the firewood, but I really need 3 firewood packages to stay warm due to my anaemia. Let’s compromise: I’ll take 3 firewood, 1 food, and 0 water, while you take 0 firewood, 2 food, and 3 water. That way, I get what I need, and you still have some food and water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your need for firewood, but I need it for warmth too. How about we split the firewood 2-1, and I give you 1 food package for 1 water package? That way, we both get what we need without taking all the wood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need 3 firewood packages to stay warm due to my anaemia. I’m willing to trade 1 food package for 1 water package if you agree to this split. Let’s finalize it now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need firewood for warmth due to my hypothyroidism. If you take 3 firewood, I’d need at least 2 food and 1 water to compensate. Let’s make this fair for both of us.
